spec_match.rationale: Implements a high-fidelity DOM capture to 2D canvas at multiple resolutions (1x and 2x), showcasing standard `toBlob()` and download workflows.

interactive.rationale: Offers real receipt editing (note, recipient name), a functional capture trigger, and a dynamic download PNG link.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Demonstrates the native alternative to html2canvas library perfectly, including multi-DPI retina export.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes a detailed SVG fallback via foreignObject when the API is not supported, handling low-fidelity rendering seamlessly.

references.rationale: Cites the ChromeStatus entry and standard specification references properly in the 'see also' section.

design_system.rationale: Follows design rules with clear warning notices, consistent typography, and shared CSS layout elements.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate form validation or dynamic state capture
openQuestions.detail: The input is fully editable, but checking/unchecking states, select dropdowns, or disabled inputs are not shown. It would be highly beneficial to add a checkbox or select option to show that complex interactive controls are pixel-perfectly rasterized by Chrome.

summary: A very practical, well-implemented concept page that highlights retina screenshot export as a superior, native alternative to third-party JS compositors, passing all spec, design, and reference checks.
